title: tephrocactus lagopus
intro: Tephrocactus lagopus, also known as the woolly prickly pear, is a type of cactus native to the Americas, characterized by its thick, woolly stems and small, white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions due to its high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Tephrocactus lagopus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, and can be found in some traditional medicine markets and online stores.
